message:
fix(security): add WebSocket origin validation to prevent CSRF attacks

The WebSocket upgrader's CheckOrigin function was returning true for all
origins with a misleading comment that "Origin checking is handled by CORS
middleware". However, CORS middleware does NOT apply to WebSocket upgrade
requests - browsers allow cross-origin WebSocket connections by default.

This was a WebSocket CSRF vulnerability where a malicious website could
establish WebSocket connections to the NTM server while authenticated users
visited the site, potentially allowing unauthorized control of agent sessions.

Added checkWSOrigin() method that:
- Allows any origin in local auth mode (development convenience)
- Allows requests without Origin header (non-browser clients)
- Validates Origin against configured corsAllowedOrigins for production modes

The check is performed in handleWebSocket before the upgrade, returning a
403 Forbidden error if the origin is not allowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
